column1: Jacob Wilson sits atop today’s Beat The Streak board, leading off at home with a strong 74% hit probability and a juicy platoon advantage against Noah Schultz—expect the steady bat and top-of-order role to pay off in a favorable park. Luis Arraez follows closely, bringing his signature contact skills and a prime No. 2 spot to a solid matchup versus Miles Mikolas, also with the platoon edge. Shohei Ohtani and Freddie Freeman both benefit from hitter-friendly conditions and favorable matchups in Colorado, making them intriguing options for those seeking extra upside.
